name: pulp-glue

metadata.name: pulp-glue
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v2 or later (GPLv2+)
metadata.summary: Version agnostic glue library to talk to pulpcore's REST API.

documentation:
# Pulp Glue
## The version agnostic Pulp 3 client library in python

functions.name: pulp_glue.common.i18n.get_translation
documentation:
Return a translations object for a certain import path.

Parameters:
    name: An import path.

Returns:
    A `gettext` translations object containing all the `gettext` variations.

Examples:
    Import the usual suspects like this:
    ```
    from pulp_glue.common.i18n import get_translation

    translation = get_translation(__package__)
    _ = translation.gettext
    ```
Need to call lru_cache() before using it as a decorator for python 3.7 compatibility

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.authentication.OAuth2ClientCredentialsAuth
documentation:
This implements the OAuth2 ClientCredentials Grant authentication flow.
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-4.4

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.OpenAPIError
documentation: Base Exception for errors related to using the openapi spec.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.OpenAPIValidationError
documentation: Exception raised for failed client side validation of parameters or request bodies.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.UnsafeCallError
documentation: Exception raised for POST, PUT, PATCH or DELETE calls with `safe_calls_only=True`.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.AuthProviderBase

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.AuthProviderBase.basic_auth
documentation: Implement this to provide means of http basic auth.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.AuthProviderBase.http_auth
documentation: Select a suitable http auth scheme or return None.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.AuthProviderBase.oauth2_client_credentials_auth
documentation: Implement this to provide other authentication methods.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.AuthProviderBase.oauth2_auth
documentation: Select a suitable oauth2 flow or return None.

Base class for auth providers.

This abstract base class will analyze the authentication proposals of the openapi specs.
Different authentication schemes should be implemented by subclasses.
Returned auth objects need to be compatible with `requests.auth.AuthBase`.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.BasicAuthProvider
documentation: Implementation for AuthProviderBase providing basic auth with fixed `username`, `password`.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.OpenAPI

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.openapi.OpenAPI.call
documentation:
Make a call to the server.

Parameters:
    operation_id: ID of the operation in the openapi v3 specification.
    parameters: Arguments that are to be sent as headers, querystrings or part of the URI.
    body: Body payload for POST, PUT, PATCH calls.
    validate_body: Indicate whether the body should be validated.

Returns:
    The JSON decoded server response if any.

Raises:
    OpenAPIValidationError: on failed input validation (no request was sent to the server).
    requests.HTTPError: on failures related to the HTTP call made.

The abstraction Layer to interact with a server providing an openapi v3 specification.

Parameters:
    base_url: The base URL inlcuding the HTTP scheme, hostname and optional subpaths of the
        served api.
    doc_path: Path of the json api doc schema relative to the `base_url`.
    headers: Dictionary of additional request headers.
    auth_provider: Object that returns requests auth objects according to the api spec.
    cert: Client certificate used for auth.
    key: Matching key for `cert` if not already included.
    validate_certs: Whether to check server TLS certificates agains a CA.
    refresh_cache: Whether to fetch the api doc regardless.
    safe_calls_only: Flag to disallow issuing POST, PUT, PATCH or DELETE calls.
    debug_callback: Callback that will be called with strings useful for logging or debugging.
    user_agent: String to use in the User-Agent header.
    cid: Correlation ID to send with all requests.

methods.name: pulp_glue.core.context.PulpUploadContext.upload_file
documentation: Upload a file and return the uncommitted upload_href.

functions.name: pulp_glue.common.context.api_quirk
documentation:
A function decorator to allow manipulating API specs based on the availability of plugins.

Parameters:
    req: The plugin specifier to determine when the quirk should be applied.

Examples:
    ```
    @api_quirk(PluginRequirement("catdog", specifier="<1.5.2"))
    def patch_barking_filter_type(api: OpenAPI) -> None:
        # fixup api.api_spec here
    ```

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PluginRequirement
documentation:
A class to represent a Pulp plugin with a set of versions.

This can be used in conjunction with `has_plugin(s)`, `needs_plugin(s)` and `CAPABILITIES`.

Parameters:
    name: The app-label of the pluin as reported by the status API.
    feature: A string being displayed as the feature if used with `needs_plugin` and the
        condition is not met.
    inverted: Treat the version set in specifier as inverted. If no specifier is provided, this
        describes the requirement of a plugin not being installed.
    specifier: A PEP-440 compatible version range.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpException
documentation: The base exception `pulp-glue` will emit on expected error paths.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityNotFound
documentation: Exception to signify that an entity was not found.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpHTTPError
documentation: Exception to indicate HTTP error responses.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpNoWait
documentation: Exception to indicate that a task continues running in the background.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext.echo
documentation:
Abstract function that will be called to emit warnings and task progress.

Warning:
    This function does nothing until implemented by a subclass.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext.prompt
documentation:
Abstract function that will be called to ask for a password interactively.

Note:
    If a password is provided non-interactively, this function need not be implemented.
    Doing so is deprecated.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext.from_config_files
documentation:
Create a `PulpContext` object from config files.

Note:
    This feature needs Python >=3.11 to work for now, because we don't want to add another
    dependency to pulp-glue.

Parameters:
    profile: Select a different profile from the config.
    config_locations: If provided these config files will be merged (last on wins) instead
        of the default locations.
Returns:
    A configured `PulpContext` object.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext.from_config
documentation:
Create a `PulpContext` object from a config dictionary.

Parameters:
    config: dictionary of configuration values.
Returns:
    A configured `PulpContext` object.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext.api
documentation:
The lazy evaluated `OpenAPI` object contained in this context.

This is only needed for low level interactions with the openapi spec.
All calls to the API should be performed via `call`.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext.call
documentation:
Perform an API call for operation_id.
Wait for triggered tasks to finish if not background.
Returns the operation result, or the finished task.

Parameters:
    operation_id: The operation ID in the openapi v3 spec to be called.
    non_blocking: returns unfinished tasks if `True`.
    parameters: Arguments that are to be sent as headers, querystrings or part of the URI.
    body: Body payload for POST, PUT, PATCH calls.
    validate_body: Indicate whether the body should be validated.

Returns:
    The body of the response, or the task or task group if one was issued.

Raises:
    PulpNoWait: in case the context has `background_tasks` set or a task (group) timed out.
    NotImplementedFake: if an unsafe call was attempted in `fake_mode`
    PulpHTTPError: for unhandeld REST API errors
    PulpException: for all unhandeld openapi and http connection exceptions

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext.wait_for_task
documentation:
Wait for a task to finish and return the finished task object.

Parameters:
    task: A task object to monitor.
    expect_cancel: Swaps the raising condition for completed and canceled tasks.

Raises:
    PulpNoWait: on timeout or if the context has `background_tasks` set.
    PulpException: on ctrl-c, if task failed or was canceled.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContext.wait_for_task_group
documentation:
Wait for a task group to finish and return the finished task object.

Parameters:
    task_group: A task group object all of which's tasks to monitor.

Raises:
    PulpNoWait: on timeout or if the context has `background_tasks` set.
    PulpException: on ctrl-c, if task failed or was canceled.

Abstract class for the global PulpContext object.
It is an abstraction layer for api access and output handling.

Parameters:
    api_root: The base url (excluding "api/v3/") to the servers api.
    api_kwargs: Extra arguments to pass to the wrapped `OpenAPI` object.
    background_tasks: Whether to wait for tasks. If `True`, all tasks triggered will
        immediately raise `PulpNoWait`.
    timeout: Limit of time to wait for unfinished tasks.
    domain: Name of the domain to interact with.
    fake_mode: In fake mode, no modifying calls will be performed.
        Where possible, instead of failing, the requested result will be faked.
        This implies `safe_calls_only=True` on the `api_kwargs`.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpViewSetContext

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpViewSetContext.call
documentation:
Perform an API call for operation.
Wait for triggered tasks to finish if not background.
Returns the operation result, or the finished task.

Parameters:
    operation: The operation to be performed on the entity. Usually the openapi
        operation_id is constructed by concatenating with the `ID_PREFIX`.
    non_blocking: returns unfinished tasks if `True`.
    parameters: Arguments that are to be sent as headers, querystrings or part of the URI.
    body: Body payload for POST, PUT, PATCH calls.
    validate_body: Indicate whether the body should be validated.

Returns:
    The body of the response, or the task or task group if one was issued.

Raises:
    PulpNoWait: in case the context has `background_tasks` set or a task (group) timed out.

class_variables.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpViewSetContext.ID_PREFIX
documentation: Common prefix for the operations of this entity.

class_variables.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpViewSetContext.NEEDS_PLUGINS
documentation: List of plugin requirements to operate such an entity on the server.

Base class to interact with a generic viewset.

Parameters:
    pulp_ctx: The server context to attach this viewset context to.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.scope
documentation:
Extra scope used in parameters for create and list calls.

Subclasses for nested entities can define the parameters for there parent scope here.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.entity
documentation:
Entity property that will perform a lazy lookup once it is accessed.
You can specify lookup parameters by assigning a dictionary to it,
or assign an href to the `pulp_href` property.
To reset to having no attached entity you can assign `None`.

!!!note:
    `A # type: ignore[assignment]` comment is needed due to a mypy limitation.
    https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/3004

Assigning to it will reset the lazy lookup behaviour.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.pulp_href
documentation:
Property to represent the href of the attached entity.
Assigning to it will reset the lazy lookup behaviour.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.tangible
documentation: Indicate whether an entity is available or specified by search parameters.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.preprocess_entity
documentation:
Filter to prepare the body for a create or update call.

This function can be subclassed by specific Entity contexts to fix data depending on plugin
versions.

Parameters:
    body: The payload representing the entity to create or the fields to update on it.
    partial: Should be set if `body` may only represent part of the entity.

Returns:
    The body ready to be passed to `call`.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.list_iterator
documentation:
List entities from this context in a batched iterator.

Parameters:
    parameters: Search or sorting criteria.
    offset: Number of entities to skip.
    batch_size: Size of the batches to fetch.
        Maximally BATCH_SIZE will be used.
    stats: If provided, a dictionary that will be filled with metadata:
        count: Number of entities reported by the server to match the criteria.

Returns:
    Iterator of entities matching the search conditions.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.list
documentation:
List entities by the type of this context.

Parameters:
    limit: Maximal number of entities to return
        Use 0 to loop until all entries are retrieved.
    offset: Number of entities to skip at the front of the list.
    parameters: Additional search or sorting criteria.

Returns:
    List of entities matching the conditions.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.find
documentation:
Find an entity based on search parameters.

Note: It is preferred to use the `entity` property instead of calling `find` directly.

Parameters:
    kwargs: The search parameters.

Returns:
    The entity if one was found uniquely.

Raises:
    PulpEntityNotFound: if no entity satisfies the search parameters.
    PulpException: if multiple entities satisfy the search parameters.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.show
documentation:
Retrieve and return the full record of an entity from the server.

Parameters:
    href: href of the entity to fetch. If not specified, the entity represented by `entity`
        will be used.

Returns:
    The full record of the entity as reported by the server.

Raises:
    PulpException: if no href was specified and the lazy lookup failed.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.create
documentation:
Create an entity.

Parameters:
    body: Fields off the new entity and the values they should be set to.
    parameters: Additional parameters for the call (usually not needed).
    non_blocking: Whether the result of the operation should be awaited on.

Returns:
    The created entity, or the record of the create task if non_blocking.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.update
documentation:
Update the entity.

Parameters:
    body: Fields and the values they should be changed to.
    parameters: Additional parameters for the call (usually not needed).
    non_blocking: Whether the result of the operation should be awaited on.

Returns:
    The updated entity, or the record of the update task if non_blocking.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.delete
documentation:
Delete the entity.

Parameters:
    non_blocking: Whether the result of the operation should be awaited on.

Returns:
    The record of the delete task.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.set_label
documentation:
Set a label.

Parameters:
    key: Name of the label.
    value: Value of the label.
    non_blocking: Whether the result of the operation should be awaited on.
        This no longer relevant for pulpcore>=3.34, because the synchronous api is used.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.unset_label
documentation:
Unset a label.

Parameters:
    key: Name of the label.
    non_blocking: Whether the result of the operation should be awaited on.
        This no longer relevant for pulpcore>=3.34, because the synchronous api is used.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.show_label
documentation:
Show value of a label.

Parameters:
    key: Name of the label.

Returns:
    Value of the label or None.

Raises:
    PulpException: if the label was not set.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.converge
documentation:
Converge an entity to have a set of desired attributes.

This will look for the entity, and depending on what it found and what should be, create,
delete or update the entity.

Parameters:
    desired_attributes: Dictionary of attributes the entity should have. `None` if the
        entity is supposed to be absent.
    defaults: Optional dict with default and extra values to be used when creating a new
        entity.

Returns:
    Tuple of (changed, before, after)

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.capable
documentation:
Report on a capability based on the presence of all needed server plugins.

Parameters:
    capability: Name of a capability.

Returns:
    Whether the capability is provided for this context.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.needs_capability
documentation:
Translates a capability in calls to `needs_plugin` via `CAPABILITIES`.

Parameters:
    capability: Name of a capability.

class_variables.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.ENTITY
documentation: Translatable name of the entity.

class_variables.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.ENTITIES
documentation: Translatable plural of `ENTITY`.

class_variables.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.HREF
documentation: Name of the href parameter in the url patterns.

class_variables.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.NULLABLES
documentation: Set of fields that can be cleared by sending 'null'.

class_variables.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.CAPABILITIES
documentation:
List of capabilities this entity provides.

Subclasses can specify version dependent capabilities here

Example:
    ```
    CAPABILITIES = {
        "feature1": [
            PluginRequirement("file"),
            PluginRequirement("core", specifier=">=3.7.0")
        ]
    }
    ```

class_variables.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpEntityContext.HREF_PATTERN
documentation: Regular expression with capture groups for 'plugin' and 'resource_type' to match URIs.

Base class for entity specific contexts.
This class provides the basic CRUD commands and ties its instances to the global
PulpContext for api access.
It typically corresponds to a NamedModelViewset.
Mostly specification is achieved by defining / extending the class attributes below.

Parameters:
    pulp_ctx: The server context to attach this entity context to.
    pulp_href: Specifying this is equivalent to assinging to `pulp_href` later.
    entity: Specifying this is equivalent to assinging to `entity` later.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpRemoteContext
documentation: Base class for remote contexts.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpPublicationContext
documentation: Base class for publication contexts.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpDistributionContext
documentation: Base class for distribution contexts.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpRepositoryVersionContext

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpRepositoryVersionContext.repair
documentation:
Trigger a repair task for this repository version.

Returns:
    The record of the repair task.

Base class for repository version contexts.

Parameters:
    pulp_ctx: The server context to attach this entity to.
    repository_ctx: Context of the repository this context should be scoped to.
    pulp_href: Specifying this is equivalent to assinging to `pulp_href` later.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpRepositoryContext

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpRepositoryContext.get_version_context
documentation:
Return a repository version context of the proper type scoped for this repository.

Parameters:
    number: Version number or `-1` for the latest version.

Returns:
    Repository version context attached to the same pulp_ctx and scoped to the repository.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpRepositoryContext.sync
documentation:
Trigger a sync task for this repository.

Parameters:
    body: Any additional options specific to the repository type used to perform this sync.

Returns:
    Record of the sync task.

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpRepositoryContext.modify
documentation:
Add to or remove content from this repository.

Parameters:
    add_content: List of content hrefs to add.
    remove_content: List of content hrefs to remove.
    base_version: Href to a repository version relative to whose content the changes are to
        be interpreted.

Returns:
    Record of the modify task.
Base class for repository contexts.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpGenericRepositoryContext

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpGenericRepositoryContext.reclaim
documentation:
Reclaim disk space for a list of repositories.

Parameters:
    repo_hrefs: List of repository hrefs to reclaim.
    repo_versions_keeplist: List of repository version hrefs to keep unaffected.

Returns:
    Record of the reclaim space task.
Generic repository context class to separate specific general functionality.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContentContext

methods.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContentContext.upload
documentation:
Create a content unit by uploading a file.

This function is deprecated. The create call can handle the upload logic transparently.

Parameters:
    file: A file like object that supports `os.path.getsize`.
    chunk_size: Size of the chunks to upload independently.
    repository: Repository context to add the newly created content to.
    kwargs: Extra args specific to the content type, passed to the create call.

Returns:
    The result of the create task.
Base class for content contexts.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpACSContext
documentation: Base class for ACS contexts.

classes.name: pulp_glue.common.context.PulpContentGuardContext
documentation: Base class for content guard contexts.
